$metadata.name: BELLA
$metadata.note: Flat rollup of primitive + semantic(light) + component. Dark mode lives in bella.css as [data-theme='dark'] overrides.

ink.$description: Signature near-black with a blue-cool undertone. The definitive dark surface and high-contrast text color for BELLA.

dont: Do not use as body text color on dark surfaces — use parchment instead
Do not use pure #000 — ink is warmer and less harsh
bella.a11y: Passes WCAG AAA against parchment (~17:1) and neutral-100 (~15:1). Fails against amber and steel — do not combine as text on those.

parchment.$description: Signature warm off-white. The default page background for BELLA. Replaces pure white everywhere.

usage: Default canvas for cards, sections, layouts
dont: Never replace with pure #FFFFFF — warmth is part of the brand
Do not use as a card surface on parchment pages — use neutral-100 for subtle elevation
bella.a11y: Passes WCAG AAA against ink, slate, and neutral-900 (≥15:1). Fails against amber — do not use as text on amber buttons (use ink).

amber.$description: Warm gold accent. The single brand accent — used as background, decoration, and (on ink only) as text.

usage: Primary CTA button background (text sits on it, in ink)
Alpha-tinted backgrounds for tags and chips (via amber-15 / amber-30)
Borders and underline accents on editorial emphasis
Text on ink or slate surfaces in dark mode
dont: Never text on parchment — 2.44:1 fails every WCAG text threshold
Do not use for error, success, or info states — those have dedicated supporting colors
Do not saturate a screen with amber — one or two accent moments per view
bella.a11y: Verified contrast on parchment: 2.44:1 (fails all WCAG text thresholds). On ink: 7.06:1 (AAA for normal text). Amber is never text on parchment — use ink. Safe as text only on ink or neutral-900 surfaces.

slate.$description: Raised card surface on ink backgrounds. One step lighter than ink, same blue-cool family.

usage: Modal and dialog surfaces in dark mode
dont: Do not use on light backgrounds — contrast with parchment is strong but undifferentiated from ink visually
Do not use as text color — use ink or neutral-900 instead

steel.$description: Muted blue. Reserved for info-state chip washes and decorative accents. Not a text color on parchment.

usage: Dark-mode accents where contrast is verified per surface
dont: Never text on parchment — 2.88:1 fails all WCAG text thresholds
Do not use as a link color — links are ink
Do not combine steel and amber in the same tight cluster (competing warms/cools)
bella.a11y: Verified contrast on parchment: 2.88:1 (fails all WCAG text thresholds). Use only as chip wash or decorative accent — never as text or link.

dusk.$description: Muted purple. Decorative accent and icon color only — not a text color on parchment.

usage: Chip wash for category taxonomies (via alpha)
dont: Never text on parchment — 4.28:1 fails all WCAG text thresholds
Do not use as link or body color
bella.a11y: Verified contrast on parchment: 4.28:1. Passes 3:1 non-text minimum (icons, borders). Fails all text thresholds. Never use as text.

sage.$description: Muted green. Reserved for success-state decoration — icons, chip washes, borders. Not a text color on parchment.

dont: Never text on parchment — 4.35:1 fails all WCAG text thresholds
Do not use for generic 'good' decoration — sage carries explicit success semantics
bella.a11y: Verified contrast on parchment: 4.35:1. Passes 3:1 non-text minimum (icons, borders). Fails all text thresholds. Never use as text.

linen.$description: Warm light gray. Default border color on parchment backgrounds.

dont: Do not use as a background — too close to parchment to create surface separation
Do not use as text — too low contrast

50.$description: Near-white with a faint warm undertone. The closest BELLA comes to pure white — still warm.

usage: Elevated card surfaces that need to feel brighter than parchment
dont: Do not use as the default page background — that's parchment
Do not use when you really need pure white — BELLA avoids #FFFFFF by design

100.$description: Subtle warm gray. Slightly darker than parchment — used for raised surfaces where a gentle step is needed.

dont: Do not use with linen borders on parchment backgrounds — three near-identical warm grays create mud
Not a brand color — prefer linen on warm backgrounds
Do not use for primary body text on parchment — contrast 3.2:1, fails AA

500.$description: Neutral gray. AA contrast on parchment — superseded by neutral.600 for AAA text roles.

usage: Icon fills and decorative accents where AA suffices
Non-text UI where the 3:1 minimum applies
dont: Not a text color at the AAA bar — use neutral.600 for text-secondary
bella.a11y: Verified 4.86:1 on parchment — AA only, fails AAA. Do not use as text-secondary at AAA bar; use neutral.600 instead.

600.$description: Dark neutral gray. The AAA-safe secondary text color on parchment.

usage: Captions, footnotes, form helpers at the AAA bar
bella.a11y: Verified 7.12:1 on parchment — the minimum AAA gray. Use for text-secondary, captions, footnotes.

700.$description: Near-black neutral. Alternative to ink when a flatter black is needed.

usage: Body text as an alternative to neutral-900 for very long-form reading

900.$description: Darkest neutral. The default primary text color on light surfaces.

dont: Do not use interchangeably with ink as a background — ink is warmer
bella.a11y: Passes WCAG AAA against parchment (~17.4:1).

amber-15.$description: Amber at 15% alpha. For tag and chip backgrounds on light surfaces.

usage: Tag background behind amber foreground text

amber-30.$description: Amber at 30% alpha. For tag and chip borders.

steel-15.$description: Steel at 15% alpha. For info chip backgrounds.

steel-30.$description: Steel at 30% alpha. For info chip borders.

ink-6.$description: Ink at ~6% alpha. Subtle borders and dividers on light surfaces when linen is too warm.

parchment-6.$description: Parchment at ~6% alpha. Subtle borders on ink/slate surfaces in dark mode.

glass-28.$description: White at 28% alpha. Bottom stop of the lightest glass gradient.

bella.exception: Pure white alpha is permitted ONLY as a translucent overlay. Never as a solid fill. The warmth comes from parchment showing through.
Pure white alpha is permitted ONLY as a translucent overlay. Never as a solid fill. The warmth comes from parchment showing through.
Pure white alpha is permitted ONLY as a translucent overlay.
Pure white alpha is permitted ONLY as a translucent overlay.

glass-72.$description: White at 72% alpha. The top highlight of a frosted panel.

bella.exception: Pure white alpha is permitted ONLY as a translucent overlay.

glass-82.$description: White at 82% alpha. Top stop of the primary glass gradient.

bella.exception: Pure white alpha is permitted ONLY as a translucent overlay.

glass-90.$description: White at 90% alpha. Crisp inset top highlight on heavy glass.

bella.exception: Pure white alpha is permitted ONLY as a translucent overlay.

shadow-warm-03.$description: Espresso ink (#2C1810) at 3% alpha. Cognac drop shadow.

slate-60.$description: Slate at 60% alpha. Top stop of dark-mode glass.

slate-48.$description: Slate at 48% alpha. Mid dark-mode glass.

slate-32.$description: Slate at 32% alpha. Light dark-mode glass.

1.$description: Smallest step. Tight clusters, icon margins.

2.$description: Tight gap. Between related items in a cluster.

3.$description: Small gap. Card internal padding on compact items.

4.$description: Base step. Default spacing between text elements.

6.$description: Standard section gap. Used between related blocks.

usage: Gap between section and its subsections
Page section padding vertical on mobile

12.$description: Section-level gap. Between major page sections.

usage: Between hero and first content section

touch-target.$description: WCAG 2.5.5 AAA touch-target minimum. Floor for any interactive element on touch devices.

usage: min-height / min-width for buttons, inputs, tags, nav-links on touch
dont: Do not go below 44px on any touchable element — fails WCAG 2.5.5 AAA
bella.a11y: WCAG 2.5.5 Target Size (Enhanced) AAA requires ≥44×44px.

touch-target-comfortable.$description: Recommended touch-target for primary actions — a step beyond the AAA minimum.

mobile.$description: Mobile breakpoint. Single-column layouts below this width.

usage: max-width media query for phone layouts

tablet.$description: Tablet breakpoint. Two-column grids become feasible from here.

usage: min-width media query for tablet and up

desktop.$description: Desktop breakpoint. Standard multi-column editorial layouts.

usage: min-width media query for desktop layouts

wide.$description: Wide-desktop breakpoint. Editorial max-width and hero-scale type sizes.

usage: min-width media query for large screens

lg.$description: Large radius. Cards and raised surfaces.

xl.$description: Extra-large radius. card.default, inner panels, hero images.

3xl.$description: 3× large radius. Feature / hero glass surfaces.

display.$description: Display and body typeface. Editorial serif — Georgia — chosen for warmth and confidence. Distinctive against generic sans-serif AI aesthetic.

dont: Do not pair with another serif as display
Do not use for UI microcopy at <13px — Georgia loses legibility below that

body.$description: Body typeface — currently identical to display (Georgia). Kept as a separate token in case body later splits to a distinct face.

mono.$description: Monospace typeface. JetBrains Mono — geometric, technical, distinctive. Used for tags, labels, eyebrows, and code.

dont: Do not use for body copy
Do not use below 13px

tag.$description: Floor for mono tags and small labels. Never go smaller.

dont: Never below 13px anywhere in BELLA

sm.$description: Small serif. Secondary captions and footnotes only.

dont: Do not use for body copy

base.$description: Body text floor. Non-negotiable minimum for reading text in BELLA.

dont: Never below 16px for body

lg.$description: Large body. Lead paragraphs, long-form reading comfort.

dont: Do not use for body or card titles — too heavy

usage: Small mono labels without full caps treatment

wider.$description: Very loose tracking. For uppercase mono eyebrows.

usage: All standard hover and focus transitions

dont: Do not use on text transitions — feels unstable

hover-lift.$description: Default hover elevation delta for cards.

xs.$description: Lightest frost. Hover hints, subtle backdrops.

sm.$description: Small frost. Content cards, modal backdrop.

lg.$description: Large frost. Primary glass card / hero panel.

sm.$description: Faint shadow. Resting card elevation on parchment.

hover.$description: Card hover shadow — stronger than sm, lighter than lg.

usage: Card hover state, paired with motion.transform.hover-lift

soft.$description: 3-layer warm shadow. Default floating elevation for hero / feature glass.

layered.$description: 4-layer shadow — 3 drops + inset top highlight. Primary glass card.

card-default.$description: 2-layer drop + inset top highlight. Subtle content-panel elevation.

card-elevated.$description: 2-layer drop + inset top highlight. Elevated case-study card.

background.$description: Default page background in light mode.

dont: Do not replace with pure white

background-inverse.$description: Inverse dark section on a light page. Used for dark-mode demo blocks inside light pages.

usage: full-bleed dark sections embedded in a light layout

surface.$description: Resting raised surface on parchment. Cards sit on this.

surface-elevated.$description: Higher-elevation surface. For modals or cards that need to feel brighter than surface.

surface-inverse.$description: Raised surface on dark backgrounds (dark-mode embedded blocks in light mode).

surface-glass.$description: Primary glass surface. Parchment shows through the warm cream. Pair with border-glass-top, shadow.raised, and blur.surface.

a11y.contextRequirement: Parent background must be parchment or lighter. Text on glass: ink only. Do not stack glass over amber/steel/dusk/sage.
Parent background must be parchment or lighter. Text on glass: ink only. Do not stack glass over amber/steel/dusk/sage.

surface-glass-light.$description: Lightest glass. Subtle secondary panels and nested containers.

a11y.contextRequirement: Parent background must be parchment or lighter. Text on glass: ink only. Do not stack glass over amber/steel/dusk/sage.

border-glass-top.$description: Top highlight on a frosted panel — mimics natural light on glass.

text-primary.$description: Primary text color on light surfaces.

bella.a11y: AAA against background and surface.
AAA against parchment (7.12:1). The AAA-safe secondary gray — safe for captions, footnotes, and supporting copy at the AAA bar.

dont: Never primary body text — fails AA

text-inverse.$description: Text on dark surfaces. Used inside background-inverse blocks.

usage: headings and body inside slate cards

text-accent.$description: Accent/emphasis text color — resolves to ink-equivalent on parchment. Amber fails AAA as text (2.44:1); emphasis carries via weight, italic, or a tinted background, not letter color.

dont: Do not swap back to amber — 2.44:1 fails every WCAG text threshold on parchment

accent.$description: Primary accent — used as background on CTAs and as wash on chips. Text sits on accent, not as accent.

dont: Never as text color on parchment — amber is 2.44:1

accent-border.$description: Amber border. Tag and chip borders.

border-subtle.$description: Near-invisible border. Use when linen is too warm.

link.$description: Default link color — same as body text. Link affordance is underline, not color. Steel was 2.88:1 on parchment and fails AAA.

dont: Do not color-only distinguish links — always pair with underline or weight

link-hover.$description: Link hover — same color. Hover affordance carries via text-decoration-thickness bump or font-weight shift, not color change.

dont: Do not swap back to amber on hover — fails AAA

success.$description: Success state color — decorative / icon / border use only (sage fails AAA as text on parchment).

info.$description: Info state color — decorative / chip-wash / border use only (steel fails AAA as text on parchment).

background.$description: Default page background in dark mode.

background-inverse.$description: Inverse light section inside a dark page.

usage: full-bleed light sections embedded in a dark layout

surface.$description: Raised surface on ink. Cards sit on this in dark mode.

usage: card default background in dark mode

surface-elevated.$description: Higher-elevation surface in dark mode. One step lighter than slate.

surface-inverse.$description: Raised surface for light sections embedded in dark pages.

surface-glass.$description: Dark glass. Slate at 60% alpha over ink — warm shadows remain.

a11y.contextRequirement: Parent background must be ink or darker. Text on glass: parchment only. Amber passes AAA (7.06:1) for emphasis spans.

surface-glass-elevated.$description: Mid dark glass. Elevated cards and content panels.

a11y.contextRequirement: Parent background must be ink or darker. Text on glass: parchment only.

surface-glass-light.$description: Lightest dark glass. Subtle nested panels in dark mode.

a11y.contextRequirement: Parent background must be ink or darker. Text on glass: parchment only.

surface-glass-amber.$description: Amber-tinted glass — active-state only. Amber at 15% over slate.

usage: Active nav-link glass background in dark mode

a11y.contextRequirement: Only use for active or selected state backgrounds. Never as a default surface. Text on amber-glass: parchment only.

bella.a11y: AAA against ink.

text-secondary.$description: Secondary text — warm neutral tuned for dark mode.

bella.a11y: AA against slate.

text-muted.$description: Muted text in dark mode. Metadata.

dont: Never body text

text-inverse.$description: Text on light surfaces inside dark pages.

text-accent.$description: Amber text. Eyebrows and emphasis — only on ink backgrounds where AAA holds.

usage: eyebrows, highlight spans in dark mode
bella.a11y: AAA against ink (7.06:1) at 16px+.

accent.$description: Primary accent. Same amber, both modes.

accent-subtle.$description: Amber wash — works on ink because alpha is transparent.

border.$description: Default border in dark mode. Very subtle parchment wash.

border-subtle.$description: Slightly lighter than slate — for section dividers.

border-strong.$description: High-contrast border in dark mode. For focus.

link.$description: Default link color in dark mode — parchment. Affordance via underline, not color.

link-hover.$description: Link hover — amber in dark mode where it passes AAA against ink (7.06:1).

bella.name: Button
bella.description: Primary call-to-action. Warm amber background for primary, ink text across variants. Reserved for the single most important action in a view.
dont: Do not use more than one primary button per view
Do not stack two buttons of the same variant
bella.a11y: All variants use ink foreground (neutral.900) for AAA on parchment (17.2:1). Secondary keeps the amber border — amber as decoration, ink as text. Focus ring uses border-strong at 2px offset. Hit area minimum 44×44px — encoded as min-height on every variant.

bella.name: Card
bella.description: Editorial glass-first card system. Three primary tiers progress from subtle panel (default) to hero glass (glass). Plus featured (solid bright) and inverse (dark-mode embed).
dont: Do not nest cards inside cards — glass-on-glass becomes mud
Do not use for navigation — that's nav-link's job
Do not place glass over amber/steel/dusk/sage — the contrast model breaks
bella.a11y: Glass tiers inherit contrast from the background beneath — parent must be parchment or lighter (light mode) or ink or darker (dark mode). Text on glass: ink (light) or parchment (dark) only. If the whole card is a link, include aria-labelledby referencing the card title.

bella.name: Tag
bella.description: Uppercase mono label. Categorizes a card, a project, a mood. Ink-on-amber-wash is the canonical AAA-safe look.
dont: Do not set foreground to amber/steel/dusk/sage on parchment — those fail AAA
Do not place on amber backgrounds without verifying the foreground contrast
bella.a11y: 13px floor. Foreground is ink (AAA against parchment and amber-15 wash). min-height hits 44px — tap area via padding so the visual chip can stay small.

bella.name: Eyebrow
bella.description: Small mono label above a heading. Signals context or category.
dont: Do not use as a heading — eyebrows complement a heading, never replace one
Do not hard-code amber as the foreground — flow through text-accent so AAA enforcement stays centralized
bella.a11y: 13px floor. Foreground flows through semantic.text-accent (ink on parchment, amber on ink) for AAA in both modes.

bella.name: Input
bella.description: Text input. Serif-on-surface, linen border, amber focus ring.
dont: Do not strip the border on default state — it's how inputs are recognizable
bella.a11y: Focus ring is 2px border-strong offset 1px. Label required, never use placeholder as label. min-height hits 44px for touch.

bella.name: Link
bella.description: Inline text link inside body copy.
dont: Do not use for primary navigation — that's nav-link
Do not rely on color alone — always underlined or weight-shifted on hover
bella.a11y: Ink foreground on parchment passes AAA (17.2:1). Hover affordance is text-decoration-thickness or font-weight, not color.

bella.name: Nav Link
bella.description: Top-level navigation item. Mono, uppercase-lite. Active state reads as ink text with a 2px amber underline — amber is decoration, ink carries the letters.
dont: Do not mix nav-link and inline-link in the same cluster
Do not color-distinguish the active state with amber text — use the amber underline token instead
bella.a11y: min-height hits 44px — critical for mobile nav. Active variant uses ink text (neutral.900, AAA 17.2:1 on parchment) with a 2px amber border-bottom as the affordance — amber stays non-text decoration.

bella.name: Section
bella.description: A vertical slice of a page. Defines standard padding and gap for content blocks.
dont: Do not nest sections inside sections

bella.name: Modal
bella.description: Modal dialog scaffold. Backdrop blur over the page content, centered dialog surface above.
dont: Do not nest modals
Do not use for non-blocking UI — use a drawer or popover instead
bella.a11y: Focus trap required. Escape to close. aria-modal='true'. Backdrop blur must be gated behind prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference for AAA 2.3.3 compliance.
